implemented: Closed Tier A policy covers control, runtime snapshots, registries, mappings, recipes, queue DB, package metadata, and dataset/model-lineage manifests.
Backup creation inventories exact non-symlink files, uses the SQLite backup API for the queue, hashes every payload, self-seals the manifest, and atomically promotes a completed backup directory.
Verification rejects manifest, file-set, size, hash, policy, tier, and SQLite-integrity drift.
Restore-test requires an empty root, verifies before copying, rechecks every restored hash and required category, and reports semantic replay and lineage query as explicitly unexecuted.
remaining_acceptance: Add the governed backup create/verify/restore-test CLI and separately configured real backup destination.
Run a drained live Tier A backup and clean-root restore on a separate destination, never D: while that volume is absent.
Execute registry rebuild, exact recipe semantic replay, one package verification, and one dataset/model lineage query against the restored root.
Record restore duration, missing-file result, and full evidence hash before activation.
claim_boundary: This proves an isolated Tier A byte/hash/SQLite restore foundation only. It does not use a real backup destination, run a live restore, execute semantic replay or lineage recovery, re-enable scheduled maintenance, or complete MF-P9-12.04.
